items.title: Migrate codebase from Python 2 to Python 3
items.body:
Both `main.py` and `experimental.py` used Python 2-only constructs (`izip`, `print` statements, `raw_input`, unguarded `map()`). This migrates the codebase to Python 3.

### `main.py`
- Drop `izip` import; use built-in `zip`
- `print "..."` → `print(...)`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`
- `map(int, ...)` → `list(map(int, ...))` (map is lazy in Py3)
- Integer division: `/` → `//`

### `experimental.py`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`

### `README.md`
- Requirement updated from Python 2 → Python 3
- Commands updated from `python` → `python3`

items.title: Add tox-based CI test matrix, fix lint failure
items.body:
## Summary

- Fix ruff UP035 lint failure: import `Generator` from `collections.abc` instead of `typing` in `tests/conftest.py`
- Add `search` deps to tox `cov` environment to match default `testenv`
- Add `.github/workflows/test.yml` with tox-based Python 3.10-3.14 matrix using `tox-gh-actions`

## Details

The project had tox environments and a `[gh-actions]` mapping configured but no CI workflow to run them. This adds a minimal workflow that:
- Installs `git-annex` (needed by tests)
- Installs `.[ci]` extras (tox + tox-uv + tox-gh-actions)
- Runs `tox` which `tox-gh-actions` automatically scopes to the right envs per Python version

On Python 3.12: runs tests + lint + type + cov. On all other versions: runs tests only.

## Test plan

- [x] `tox -e lint` passes locally
- [x] `tox -e type` passes locally
- [x] `tox -e py3` passes locally (420 tests)
- [x] CI workflow runs successfully on the PR itself

items.title: Fix CI #16: resolve pylint violations and Python 3.13 compatibility
items.body:
CI #16 failed due to pylint violations across 6 files (exit code 1, 9.46/10 score) and Python 3.13 version constraint mismatch. All issues are resolved without disabling pylint globally.

## What's Changed

### Circular import eliminated (`diffsync/models.py` ↔ `diffsync/adapters.py`)
- `_is_zabbix_target()` previously imported `ZabbixRemoteAdapter` at call time to do `isinstance` — causing `R0401` cyclic-import and `C0415` import-outside-toplevel
- Replaced with a marker attribute check: `getattr(adapter, "IS_ZABBIX_REMOTE", False)`
- Added `IS_ZABBIX_REMOTE = True` to `ZabbixRemoteAdapter`; compatible with `MagicMock(spec=ZabbixRemoteAdapter)` in tests

### Deferred imports moved to module top-level
| File | Symbol | Pylint code |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `diffsync/models.py` | `get_zabbix_client_from_config` | C0415 (×3) |
| `diffsync/adapters.py` | `nautobot.dcim.models.Device` | C0415 |
| `jobs.py` | `django.conf.settings`, `DataMapping` | C0415 (×4) |
| `utils/zabbix.py` | `django.conf.settings` | C0415 |

Tests that patched `utils.zabbix.get_zabbix_client_from_config` for model CRUD tests updated to patch `diffsync.models.get_zabbix_client_from_config` — the correct location after top-level import.

### Inline pylint disables for intentional patterns
- `broad-exception-caught` (W0718) on two adapter catch blocks — alongside existing `# noqa: BLE001`
- `too-few-public-methods` (R0903) on `Meta` inner classes in `jobs.py` and test class in `test_zabbix_diff.py`
- `too-many-arguments` / `too-many-locals` (R0913/R0914) on `ZabbixClient.build_interface` and `upsert_host`

### Test cleanup
- All local imports inside test methods moved to module top-level
- Empty-sequence assertion: `list(...) == []` → `not any(...)` (C1803)

### Python 3.13 support (`pyproject.toml` + `poetry.lock`)
- Updated `requires-python` from `>=3.10,<3.13` to `>=3.10,<3.14` to match CI matrix which tests Python 3.13
- Updated `[tool.poetry.dependencies] python` from `>=3.10,<3.13` to `>=3.10,<3.14`
- Added `Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13` classifier
- Regenerated `poetry.lock` via `poetry lock` (v2.1.3) to reflect the updated Python version constraint

**Note**: The `unittest (3.10, postgresql, stable)` failure in CI run #18 was a transient Docker timeout pulling `ghcr.io/nautobot/nautobot-dev:stable-py3.10`, not a code issue.

## To Do

- [x] Explanation of Change(s)
- [ ] Added change log fragment(s) (for more information see [the documentation](https://docs.nautobot.com/projects/core/en/stable/development/core/#creating-changelog-fragments))
- [ ] Attached Screenshots, Payload Example
- [x] Unit, Integration Tests
- [ ] Documentation Updates (when adding/changing features)
- [ ] Outline Remaining Work, Constraints from Design

items.title: Fix/python 3.14
Upgrade psycopg2 to support Python 3.13
items.body:
## Problem

The backend crashes on startup with `ImportError: undefined symbol: _PyInterpreterState_Get` because psycopg2 2.9.9 was not compiled with Python 3.13 support, making it incompatible with the Railpack build environment which uses Python 3.13.12.

## Solution

Bumped psycopg2 from 2.9.9 to 2.9.10 in `library-app/backend/requirements.txt`. The 2.9.10 release adds official Python 3.13 support, resolving the symbol lookup error and allowing the app to connect to PostgreSQL normally.

### Changes
- **Modified** `library-app/backend/requirements.txt`

---

```
$ uchardet requirements.txt 
GB18030
````

Because of this, `pip install -r requirements.txt` no longer works.

```
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):

  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/pip/_internal/commands/install.py", line 354, in run
```

items.title: Bump Dockerfile base image to Python 3.11
items.body:
The codebase uses `str | None` union syntax (PEP 604), which requires Python 3.10+. The Dockerfile was pinned to `python:3.9-slim`, causing a startup crash via Pydantic's type hint evaluation.

## Change

- **`backend/Dockerfile`**: `python:3.9-slim` → `python:3.11-slim`

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

Update the Dockerfile to use Python 3.11 instead of Python 3.9.

**Current Issue:**
The application fails to start with this error:
```
TypeError: You have a type annotation 'str | None' which makes use of newer typing features than are supported in your version of Python.
```

This happens because the code uses Python 3.10+ syntax (`str | None`) but the Dockerfile uses Python 3.9, which doesn't support this syntax.

**Solution:**
Update the Dockerfile to use `python:3.11-slim` instead of `python:3.9-slim`. This will:
1. Support the modern type annotation syntax used in the codebase
2. Be more compatible with the Pydantic v2.x and other dependencies
3. Still be a lightweight image (slim variant)

**File to change:**
- `backend/Dockerfile` - Change `FROM python:3.9-slim` to `FROM python:3.11-slim`

items.title: Add Selenium-based NotebookLM scraper with manual-input fallback
items.body:
The Flask web interface was stuck in permanent "manual-input mode" because the unofficial NotebookLM SDK is unreliable. This adds a direct Selenium scraper as the primary fetch path and promotes manual input to a first-class, always-available fallback.

## New: `notebooklm_scraper.py`
Headless Chrome scraper that handles the full Google OAuth flow and extracts notebooks without the SDK:
- Three progressive notebook-discovery strategies: `data-notebook-id` attrs → `/notebook/<id>` hrefs → generic card elements
- All URL checks use `urlparse` hostname comparison (not substring matching) — avoids incomplete-URL-sanitization vulnerabilities
- `login()` returns `False` (never raises) when Selenium/Chrome is absent, so the caller degrades cleanly

## `app.py`
- `/api/notebooks` — tries scraper → SDK → returns `{"notebooks": [], "warning": "..."}` with a hint to use manual input
- `/api/generate` — now accepts two modes:
  ```json
  { "notebook_id": "abc123", "num_slides": 8 }          // auto: scraper → SDK
  { "notebook_text": "...", "notebook_title": "...", "num_slides": 8 }  // manual
  ```
- Background worker `_run_generation` handles both paths; auto mode falls through scraper → SDK → error

## `templates/index.html`
- Dropdown populates with real notebook titles on load; shows "Loading notebooks…" while fetching
- Manual-input section (title field + textarea) surfaces automatically when no notebooks are found
- "Enter content manually instead" / "← Back to notebook selector" toggles for explicit switching

![UI in fallback manual-input mode](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d22e5e1-c706-41d8-8815-c50b2bdd2794)

## `requirements.txt`
Added `selenium>=4.0.0`, `beautifulsoup4>=4.12.0`, `requests>=2.31.0`.

items.title: Add unified `generators.py` CLI consolidating all 43 generator/test scripts
items.body:
All root-level generator/test scripts were standalone, requiring per-script invocation with no common entrypoint, discoverability, or shared argument conventions.

## What's added

### `generators.py` — single self-contained CLI
- Argparse subcommands matching original script names (no `.py`); no imports of old scripts
- `--list` enumerates all generators with descriptions
- Seed-driven generators (`randomint`, `randomint3`, `randomint4`) take a positional `seed` arg; `freader` takes `filename start length [flen]`; all others pipe via stdin/stdout

```bash
python generators.py --list
python generators.py intcounter14 | head -5
python generators.py randomint myseed | head -3
echo "hello" | python generators.py rot13
python generators.py intcounter14 | python generators.py testxor | head -10
```

### Python 3 compatibility fixes (originals left unchanged)
Five bugs in original scripts prevent execution under Python 3; fixed in the unified version with docstring notes:

| Script | Bug | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `testinv.py` | `K` undefined (NameError) | → `k` |
| `testsha256.py` | `bytes.encode('hex')` (Py2) | → `bytes.hex()` |
| `testSTRToSHA512.py` | `str` passed to `hashlib.update()` | → `.encode('utf-8')` |
| `testpivot.py` | `/` on index (Py2 int division) | → `//` |
| `freader.py` | Py2 bytes/string mixing in entropy | Rewrote for Py3 `bytes` native API |

All other original behavior (including quirks) is preserved verbatim.

### `README.md` — new
Documents all generators, optional deps (`numpy`, `python-stdnum`), piping patterns, and the bug-fix table above.

items.title: Fix seed parameter TypeError on Python < 3.10
items.body:
The `simulate()` function used the `int | None` union type syntax (PEP 604) introduced in Python 3.10, causing a `TypeError` at import time on Python 3.9 and below.

## Changes

- **`game_of_life.py`**: Replace `seed: int | None = None` with `seed: Optional[int] = None` using `typing.Optional` for compatibility with Python 3.7+
- **`README.md`**: Fix incorrect "GPU implementation" description; add accurate project overview and CLI usage docs for both `game_of_life.py` and `render_animation.py`

```python
# Before (Python 3.10+ only)
def simulate(..., seed: int | None = None) -> None:

# After (Python 3.7+)
from typing import Optional
def simulate(..., seed: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
```

fix: resolve collectstatic failure caused by cloudinary_storage ordering and strict manifest storage
items.body:
`python manage.py collectstatic` fails on Render because `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage` validates all CSS file references, and Django 5.x's `admin/css/base.css` references `admin/img/sorting-icons.svg` — a file that isn't found due to `cloudinary_storage` being listed before `django.contrib.staticfiles` in `INSTALLED_APPS`.

## Changes

- **`INSTALLED_APPS`**: moved `django.contrib.staticfiles` before `cloudinary_storage` so Django Admin's static files are discovered before cloudinary overrides the storage backend.
- **`STATICFILES_STORAGE`**: switched from `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage` to `CompressedStaticFilesStorage` — retains compression but drops the strict CSS reference validation that was causing the build failure.

```python
# Before
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    ...
    'cloudinary_storage',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    ...
]
STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'whitenoise.storage.CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage'

# After
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    ...
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    'cloudinary_storage',
    ...
]
STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'whitenoise.storage.CompressedStaticFilesStorage'
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Problema

O build no Render falha durante o `python manage.py collectstatic --noinput` com o seguinte erro:

```
whitenoise.storage.MissingFileError: The file 'admin/img/sorting-icons.svg' could not be found with <whitenoise.storage.CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage object at 0x7e5ea2ca92b0>.

The CSS file 'admin/css/base.css' references a file which could not be found:
  admin/img/sorting-icons.svg
```

## Causa raiz

Dois problemas em `smartlibrary/settings.py`:

1. **`cloudinary_storage` está antes de `django.contrib.staticfiles`** no `INSTALLED_APPS` — isso interfere na descoberta dos arquivos estáticos do Django Admin.
2. **`STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'whitenoise.storage.CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage'`** — esse backend valida todas as referências a arquivos nos CSS. O Django 5.x adicionou referência a `admin/img/sorting-icons.svg` no `admin/css/base.css`, e a ordem incorreta do `INSTALLED_APPS` faz com que esse arquivo não seja encontrado durante a validação.

## Solução

Fazer duas correções em `smartlibrary/settings.py`:

1. Mover `django.contrib.staticfiles` para **antes** de `cloudinary_storage` no `INSTALLED_APPS`.
2. Trocar `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage` por `CompressedStaticFilesStorage` (sem o `Manifest`), que comprime os arquivos mas não faz a validação rigorosa de referências em CSS.

### Mudanças necessárias

**`INSTALLED_APPS`** — mudar de:
```python
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.messages',
    'cloudinary_storage',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    'cloudinary',
    'livros',
]
```
Para:
```python
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.messages',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    'cloudinary_storage',
    'cloudinary',
    'livros',
]
```

**`STATICFILES_STORAGE`** — mudar de:
```python
STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'whitenoise.storage.CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage'
```
Para:
```python
STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'whitenoise.storage.CompressedStaticFilesStorage'
```

items.title: Add pymorphy3 lemmatization, webhook notifications, fix code bugs, clean up repository
items.body:
Addresses several outstanding tasks: replaces the primitive suffix-stripping lemmatizer with proper morphological analysis via pymorphy3, adds a fail-safe webhook notification system, fixes five code correctness issues, expands README, adds integration tests, and removes committed build artifacts.

## Bug Fixes

- **`gemini_client.py`**: `asyncio.get_event_loop()` → `asyncio.get_running_loop()` (deprecated/broken in Python 3.12+)
- **`compliance.py`**: double `regex.search(text)` call collapsed into single `match = regex.search(text)`
- **`logging.py`**: `JsonFileHandler` gains `__del__` to guarantee file close on GC
- **`keyword_analysis.py`**: redundant `[^\w\sа-яёА-ЯЁ]` → `[^\w\s]` (`\w` already covers Cyrillic in Python 3)
- **`config.py`**: `hashlib.md5` → `hashlib.sha256` (8-char prefix) for job_id generation

## pymorphy3 Lemmatization

Replaces `simple_lemma()` suffix-stripping (which produced `"пальто" → "пальт"`) with proper morphological normalization:

```python
# Before: suffix stripper gives wrong normal forms
simple_lemma("пальто")   # → "пальт"  ❌
simple_lemma("тёплого")  # → "тёплог" ❌

# After: pymorphy3 returns correct dictionary forms
lemmatize("пальто")   # → "пальто"  ✅
lemmatize("тёплого")  # → "тёплый"  ✅
```

- Lazy singleton `MorphAnalyzer` (no import-time cost)
- Automatic fallback to `_suffix_lemma` if pymorphy3 is unavailable
- `simple_lemma()` kept as backwards-compatible alias
- `pymorphy3>=2.0,<3` added to `pyproject.toml` and `requirements.txt`

## Webhook Notifications

New `src/wb_card_gen/services/webhook.py` — `WebhookNotifier` posts JSON events on stage/job lifecycle. All methods are fail-safe (exception → log + return `False`, never interrupts pipeline):

```python
# Three event types posted to WEBHOOK_URL
await notifier.notify_stage_complete(job_id, "research", "COMPLETE")
await notifier.notify_error(job_id, "research", "Connection refused")
await notifier.notify_job_complete(job_id, "READY", {...})
```

- `WEBHOOK_URL` added to `AppSettings` and `.env.example`
- Orchestrator calls notifier after each stage and at pipeline end
- CLI `run` gains `--webhook-url` to override env value

## README Expansion

Added: Requirements section (Python 3.11+), full `pip install` command, environment variables table, Webhook payload documentation with examples, Troubleshooting section (Playwright, Gemini quota, pymorphy3 dicts, WB API). TODO updated to remove completed items.

## Repository Cleanup

- All `__pycache__/` dirs and `src/wb_card_gen.egg-info/` removed from git tracking (were already in `.gitignore`)
- Deleted `.github/agents/test` (7 bytes) and `faq/test` (1 byte) junk files
- Added `tests/integration/test_cli_integration.py` and `test_webhook_integration.py`
- Added `requirements.txt` for users not using `pip install -e`

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Контекст
Репозиторий WB_analiz — production-grade система для автоматической подготовки карточки товара Wildberries. После полного аудита кода выявлены следующие задачи, которые нужно реализовать в одном PR.

## Задачи для реализации

### 1. Добавить pymorphy3 для полноценной лемматизации
Файл `src/wb_card_gen/services/keyword_analysis.py` содержит примитивный суффиксный лемматизатор `simple_lemma()` (строки 34-50), который просто обрезает окончания. Это даёт некорректные результаты для русского языка.

**Что нужно сделать:**
- Добавить `pymorphy3>=2.0,<3` в `dependencies` в `pyproject.toml`
- В файле `src/wb_card_gen/services/keyword_analysis.py`:
  - Добавить `import pymorphy3` и создать синглтон `MorphAnalyzer` (ленивая инициализация, чтобы не замедлять импорт)
  - Создать новую функцию `lemmatize(word: str) -> str` которая использует `pymorphy3.MorphAnalyzer().parse(word)[0].normal_form`
  - Функцию `simple_lemma()` сохранить как fallback (переименовать в `_suffix_lemma`) и использовать если pymorphy3 недоступен
  - Заменить все вызовы `simple_lemma()` на новую `lemmatize()` во всех местах файла
- Обновить тесты в `tests/unit/test_keyword_analysis.py`:
  - Обновить тесты `TestSimpleLemma` для проверки pymorphy3 лемматизации (например, `lemmatize("пальто")` должен возвращать `"пальто"`, а не `"пальт"`)
  - Добавить тесты для fallback на suffix lemma

### 2. Улучшить README.md
Текущий README.md неплохой, но нуждается в расширении:

**Что нужно добавить:**
- Секцию "Требования" (Python 3.11+, системные зависимости)
- Полную команду установки всех pip-зависимостей (включая новый pymorphy3):
  ```
  pip install pydantic pydantic-settings httpx aiohttp Pillow numpy tenacity PyYAML python-dotenv structlog fake-useragent playwright playwright-stealth click rich google-genai pymorphy3
  ```
- Секцию "Переменные окружения" с описанием каждой переменной из `.env.example`
- Секцию "Webhook-уведомления" (см. п.3)
- Секцию "Troubleshooting" с типичными проблемами (установка playwright, ошибки API, pymorphy3 словари)
- Обновить TODO: убрать выполненные пункты (pymorphy3 и webhook)

### 3. Webhook-уведомления о завершении задачи
Сейчас в коде нет никакой системы уведомлений. Нужно:

**Создать файл `src/wb_card_gen/services/webhook.py`:**
- Класс `WebhookNotifier` с методами:
  - `__init__(self, webhook_url: str | None, timeout: int = 10)` 
  - `async def notify_job_complete(self, job_id: str, verdict: str, details: dict) -> bool` — отправляет POST на webhook_url с JSON-payload `{"event": "job_complete", "job_id": ..., "verdict": ..., "timestamp": ..., "details": ...}`
  - `async def notify_stage_complete(self, job_id: str, stage: str, status: str) -> bool` — уведомление о завершении отдельного этапа
  - `async def notify_error(self, job_id: str, stage: str, error: str) -> bool` — уведомление об ошибке
  - Все методы должны быть fail-safe (try/except, логировать ошибки, не прерывать основной pipeline)
  - Если `webhook_url` is None — методы молча возвращают False

**Интеграция:**
- В `src/wb_card_gen/config.py`: добавить в `AppSettings` поле `webhook_url: str = Field(default="", alias="WEBHOOK_URL")`
- В `.env.example`: добавить `WEBHOOK_URL=` 
- В `src/wb_card_gen/orchestrator.py`: создать экземпляр `WebhookNotifier` и вызывать:
  - `notify_stage_complete()` после каждого успешного этапа
  - `notify_error()` при ошибке этапа
  - `notify_job_complete()` в конце pipeline (перед return)
- В CLI (`src/wb_card_gen/cli.py`): добавить опцию `--webhook-url` для переопределения из командной строки

### 4. Проверка незавершённых задач по контракту и исправление

**4.1. `__pycache__` закоммичены в git:**
- Директории `__pycache__` не должны быть в репозитории. Хотя `.gitignore` их исключает, они уже попали в историю. В `.gitignore` добавить `*.egg-info/` если нет, и директорию `src/wb_card_gen.egg-info/` тоже не должна быть в репозитории.

**4.2. Интеграционные тесты пустые:**
- `tests/integration/__init__.py` пуст, нет ни одного интеграционного теста. Добавить хотя бы базовый интеграционный тест:
  - `tests/integration/test_cli_integration.py` — тест CLI через `click.testing.CliRunner` с dry-run
  - `tests/integration/test_webhook_integration.py` — тест webhook с mock-сервером

**4.3. Мусорные файлы:**
- `.github/agents/test` — файл размером 7 байт, мусор, удалить
- `faq/test` — файл размером 1 байт, мусор, удалить

### 5. Исправление ошибок и неточностей в коде

**5.1. `src/wb_card_gen/clients/gemini_client.py` строка 141:**
```python
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
```
`asyncio.get_event_loop()` deprecated в Python 3.12+. Заменить на:
```python
loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
```

**5.2. `src/wb_card_gen/services/compliance.py` строка 38:**
Двойной вызов `regex.search(text)` — неэффективность. Переписать:
```python
match = regex.search(text)
if match:
    issues.append(...)
```

**5.3. `src/wb_card_gen/utils/logging.py` строка 32:**
Файл открывается в `__init__` но `close()` может не вызваться корректно. Добавить `__de...

Changelog

Sourced from lxml's changelog.

4.9.4 (2023-12-19)

Bugs fixed

  • LP#2046398: Inserting/replacing an ancestor into a node's children could loop indefinitely.

  • LP#1980767, GH#379: TreeBuilder.close() could fail with a TypeError after parsing incorrect input. Original patch by Enrico Minack.

  • LP#1522052: A file-system specific test is now optional and should no longer fail on systems that don't support it.

Other changes

  • Wheels include zlib 1.3, libxml2 2.10.3 and libxslt 1.1.39 (zlib 1.2.12, libxml2 2.10.3 and libxslt 1.1.37 on Windows).

  • Built with Cython 0.29.37.

4.9.3 (2023-07-05)

Bugs fixed

  • LP#2008911: lxml.objectify accepted non-decimal numbers like ²²² as integers.

  • A memory leak in lxml.html.clean was resolved by switching to Cython 0.29.34+.

  • GH#348: URL checking in the HTML cleaner was improved. Patch by Tim McCormack.

  • GH#371, GH#373: Some regex strings were changed to raw strings to fix Python warnings. Patches by Jakub Wilk and Anthony Sottile.

Other changes

  • Wheels include zlib 1.2.13, libxml2 2.10.3 and libxslt 1.1.38 (zlib 1.2.12, libxml2 2.10.3 and libxslt 1.1.37 on Windows).

  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

Updates `playwright` from 1.45.0 to 1.58.0
Release notes

Sourced from playwright's releases.

v1.58.0

Trace Viewer Improvements

  • New 'system' theme option follows your OS dark/light mode preference
  • Search functionality (Cmd/Ctrl+F) is now available in code editors
  • Network details panel has been reorganized for better usability
  • JSON responses are now automatically formatted for readability

Thanks to @​cpAdm for contributing these improvements!

Miscellaneous

browser_type.connect_over_cdp() now accepts an is_local option. When set to True, it tells Playwright that it runs on the same host as the CDP server, enabling file system optimizations.

Breaking Changes ⚠️

  • Removed _react and _vue selectors. See locators guide for alternatives.
  • Removed :light selector engine suffix. Use standard CSS selectors instead.
  • Option devtools from browser_type.launch() has been removed. Use args=['--auto-open-devtools-for-tabs'] instead.
  • Removed macOS 13 support for WebKit. We recommend to upgrade your macOS version, or keep using an older Playwright version.

Browser Versions

  • Chromium 145.0.7632.6
  • Mozilla Firefox 146.0.1
  • WebKit 26.0

This version was also tested against the following stable channels:

  • Google Chrome 144
  • Microsoft Edge 144

v1.57.0

Chrome for Testing

Starting with this release, Playwright switches from Chromium, to using Chrome for Testing builds. Both headed and headless browsers are subject to this. Your tests should still be passing after upgrading to Playwright 1.57.

We're expecting no functional changes to come from this switch. The biggest change is the new icon and title in your toolbar.

If you still see an unexpected behaviour change, please file an issue.

On Arm64 Linux, Playwright continues to use Chromium.

Breaking Change

After 3 years of being deprecated, we removed page.accessibility from our API. Please use other libraries such as Axe if you need to test page accessibility. See our Node.js guide for integration with Axe.

New APIs

  • worker.on("console") event is emitted when JavaScript within the worker calls one of console API methods, e.g. console.log or console.dir. worker.expect_event() can be used to wait for it.
  • locator.description() returns locator description previously set with locator.describe().
  • New option steps in locator.click() and locator.drag_to() that configures the number of mousemove events emitted while moving the mouse pointer to the target element.
  • Network requests issued by Service Workers are now reported and can be routed through the BrowserContext, only in Chromium. You can opt out using the PLAYWRIGHT_DISABLE_SERVICE_WORKER_NETWORK environment variable.

items.title: Complete Python 3 migration and update build tooling to uv

- **Python 3 Migration**: Updated codebase for Python 3.12+ compatibility, including:

- **Build Tooling**: Updated to use `uv` for dependency management and running commands
  - Added `.gitignore` for `.claude/` and `.venv/` directories
  - Updated README with `uv sync` setup instructions
  - Replaced all `python` commands with `uv run python` in examples

- **Documentation**: Updated README to reflect Python 3.12+ requirements and modern dependency versions (gevent 26.x, greenlet 3.x)

This appears to be due to a syntax difference between 3.11 and 3.13. Python 3.11 does not allow a single-quote string to be nested inside a single-quote f-string, while Python 3.13 allows it. However, both versions accept a double-quote string nested inside a single-quote f-string. As such, this change should improve compatibility across different versions of Python.

items.title: Fix zero-input edge cases in ScoringEngine returning 12.5 instead of 0.0
items.body:
`test_zero_values_do_not_crash` was failing because `ScoringEngine.score()` returned `12.5` instead of `0.0` when all inputs are zero — caused by two scoring functions mishandling the all-zero case.

## Bugs fixed

- **`_score_investment_discipline`**: missing zero-guard caused `if asset_growth <= 0` to fire for `(0, 0)` inputs, returning `10.0` (+10.0 to total)
- **`_score_price_dislocation`**: missing null-check on `current_price` when `price_52w_high` is also `0` returned a non-zero neutral score (+2.5 to total)

Combined: `10.0 + 2.5 = 12.5` — exactly the observed failure.

## Fix

```python
# _score_investment_discipline
if asset_growth == 0 and ebitda_growth == 0:
    return 0.0          # was falling through to `if asset_growth <= 0: return 10.0`

# _score_price_dislocation
if not high or not current:
    return 0.0          # guards division and prevents spurious neutral score
```

items.title: Fix psycopg2 incompatibility with Python 3.13
items.body:
## Problem

The build fails during `collectstatic` with an `ImportError` because psycopg2-binary 2.9.9 ships a binary extension built against an older Python ABI that is missing `_PyInterpreterState_Get`, a symbol only present in Python 3.13+. This causes Django to fail to load the database driver and abort the build.

## Solution

Bumped psycopg2-binary from 2.9.9 to 2.9.10 in `requirements.txt`. Version 2.9.10 ships pre-built wheels compiled against Python 3.13, so the extension loads correctly and the `collectstatic` step can complete successfully.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

---

items.body:
Hello @Yolley, first of all thank you for forking and creating this package. My team is currently looking for a package that supports dynamic TTL calculation and distributed locking and `fastapi-cache2-fork` satisfies these needs. Unfortunately, some of our services are still on py3.11.

I took a quick look and the code changes to bring back py3.11 support should be minimal, e.g. `dec_hook[T] → dec_hook` which uses the TypeVar that's still available in the `coder.py` module. The rest I could found would just be config changes (`pyproject.toml`, `tox.ini`, `CI matrix`).

May I ask if you'd be open to a PR to restore Python 3.11 support? Thanks in any case!

1.3.1 (2025-03-03)

1.3.0 (2026-02-02)

⚠️ Deprecations & removals:

  • See changes to BSON and JSON serializers below:

💾 Serialization:

  • Significantly reduce the memory usage of JSON serialization
  • ⚠️ serializer='json' will no longer automatically use ultrajson if installed; it must be specified explicitly
  • ⚠️ Drop support for standalone bson codec; please install pymongo to use BSON serialization
  • Remove [bson] package extra to prevent accidentally installing it in the same environment as pymongo
  • When using BSON serialization with the filesystem backend, add a .bson file extension by default
  • Add support for orjson as a JSON serializer
    • However, see ijl/orjson#483 for potential memory issues

  • Remove [json] package extra due to multiple supported JSON libraries
  • Allow decode_content to be set to different values across multiple sessions in use at the same time

💾 Backends:

  • SQLite:
    • Use exclusive transaction locks to prevent write contention; replaces retry behavior
  • *Filesystem:
    • Add {py:class}requests_cache.backends.filesystem.LRUFileDict class to optionally limit the size of the filesystem cache. Enabled with max_cache_bytes argument.
  • Redis:
    • For maintenance/inspection methods that iterate over the cache, use SCAN and HSCAN instead of KEYS, HKEYS, and HGETALL
  • DynamoDB:

  • GridFS:

🕗 Expiration & headers:

  • Add support for Vary: Cookie

⚙️ Session settings:

  • Add autoclose option to close backend connections when the session is closed
  • Add read_only option to read existing cached responses, but not write any new responses to the cache

ℹ️ Cache convenience methods:

  • Add verify parameter to BaseCache.contains() and delete() to handle requests made with SSL verification disabled

🧩 Compatibility and packaging:

  • Add support for Python 3.13 and 3.14
  • Add compatibility with url-normalize 2.0
  • Add compatibility with FIPS-compliant systems

v1.58.0

Trace Viewer Improvements

  • New 'system' theme option follows your OS dark/light mode preference
  • Search functionality (Cmd/Ctrl+F) is now available in code editors

Miscellaneous

browser_type.connect_over_cdp() now accepts an is_local option. When set to True, it tells Playwright that it runs on the same host as the CDP server, enabling file system optimizations.

Breaking Changes ⚠️

  • Removed _react and _vue selectors. See locators guide for alternatives.

  • Mozilla Firefox 146.0.1

  • Google Chrome 144
  • Microsoft Edge 144

v1.57.0

Chrome for Testing

Starting with this release, Playwright switches from Chromium, to using Chrome for Testing builds. Both headed and headless browsers are subject to this. Your tests should still be passing after upgrading to Playwright 1.57.

We're expecting no functional changes to come from this switch. The biggest change is the new icon and title in your toolbar.

If you still see an unexpected behaviour change, please file an issue.

On Arm64 Linux, Playwright continues to use Chromium.

Breaking Change

After 3 years of being deprecated, we removed page.accessibility from our API. Please use other libraries such as Axe if you need to test page accessibility. See our Node.js guide for integration with Axe.

New APIs

  • worker.on("console") event is emitted when JavaScript within the worker calls one of console API methods, e.g. console.log or console.dir. worker.expect_event() can be used to wait for it.
  • locator.description() returns locator description previously set with locator.describe().
  • New option steps in locator.click() and locator.drag_to() that configures the number of mousemove events emitted while moving the mouse pointer to the target element.
  • Network requests issued by Service Workers are now reported and can be routed through the BrowserContext, only in Chromium. You can opt out using the PLAYWRIGHT_DISABLE_SERVICE_WORKER_NETWORK environment variable.

items.title: Update Django Kit | Python 3.14.3 | Django 6.0.3
Update numpy to support Python 3.13
items.body:
## Problem

The build fails on Python 3.13.12 because `numpy==1.26.4` is incompatible with `ml-dtypes 0.5.4`, a transitive dependency of TensorFlow. ml-dtypes requires `numpy>=2.1.0` when running on Python 3.13, causing pip to abort with a dependency conflict.

## Solution

Changed the numpy pin in `requirements.txt` from `numpy==1.26.4` to `numpy>=2.1.0`, allowing pip to resolve a compatible numpy 2.x release that satisfies both ml-dtypes and the rest of the ML stack (TensorFlow, pandas, scikit-learn).

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

items.title: fix: defer GPIO import in TB6612 to first Motor use
items.body:
`picar servo-install` crashed on import because `TB6612.py` attempted to import `RPi.GPIO`/`lgpio` at module level. Since `picar/__init__.py` eagerly imports `back_wheels` → `TB6612`, any missing GPIO library would raise `ImportError` before `main()` ever ran — even for commands that never touch GPIO.

## Changes

- **`picar/SunFounder_TB6612/TB6612.py`**: Moved the `RPi.GPIO`/`lgpio` resolution into a `_load_gpio()` helper; `GPIO` is set to `None` at module level and lazily initialized on first `Motor.__init__()` (or `test()`) call via `global GPIO; if GPIO is None: GPIO = _load_gpio()`

```python
# Before: crashes on import if no GPIO lib present
import RPi.GPIO as GPIO  # or lgpio fallback — module-level, always runs

# After: import deferred until a Motor is actually constructed
GPIO = None  # lazily initialized on first Motor instantiation

class Motor:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        global GPIO
        if GPIO is None:
            GPIO = _load_gpio()
```

`servo-install` exclusively uses the PCA9685/Servo path and never instantiates a `Motor`, so it now works regardless of whether a GPIO library is installed.

items.title: Fix pydub on Python 3.13+ and eliminate per-entry ffmpeg errors
items.body:
Two related issues: `audioop` was removed in Python 3.13 (PEP 594), breaking pydub's import entirely; and when ffmpeg is missing from PATH, the script emits `ERROR: pydub processing failed: [WinError 2]` once per Pokémon entry (~532×) instead of failing fast.

## `tools/requirements.txt`
- Add `audioop-lts; python_version>="3.13"` — LTS backport of `audioop`, no-op on older Pythons

## `tools/generate_voices.py`
- Add `HAS_FFMPEG = HAS_PYDUB and bool(shutil.which("ffmpeg") or shutil.which("avconv"))` checked once at module load
- Gate pydub processing on `HAS_PYDUB and HAS_FFMPEG`; raw-audio fallback runs silently (startup warning is sufficient)
- Suppress pydub's own `RuntimeWarning: Couldn't find ffmpeg` at import time via `warnings.catch_warnings()` — replaced by our single, OS-specific startup warning
- Startup warning block gains an `elif not HAS_FFMPEG` branch with actionable install instructions per OS

## `README.md`
- Requirements table: note Python 3.13+ support via `audioop-lts`; fix install path to `tools/requirements.txt`
- Troubleshooting table: replace vague `ffmpeg not found` row with specific rows for `[WinError 2]`, `ffmpeg not found on PATH`, and the Python 3.13 `audioop` ImportError

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Problem

On Python 3.13+, the built-in `audioop` module was removed (PEP 594). This causes `pydub` to fail to import because it depends on `audioop` internally. The error manifests as:

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'audioop'
```

followed by:

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pyaudioop'
```

Because the script catches this `ImportError` and sets `HAS_PYDUB = False`, users see a misleading warning: `"pydub is not installed"` even though pydub IS installed — it just can't load without `audioop`.

## Fix

Add `audioop-lts` as a conditional dependency in `tools/requirements.txt`. This is the community-maintained LTS port of the removed `audioop` module for Python 3.13+.

Add the following line to `tools/requirements.txt` under the audio processing section (near the `pydub` dependency):

```
audioop-lts; python_version>="3.13"
```

This ensures the dependency is only installed on Python 3.13+ where `audioop` is missing, and has no effect on older Python versions.

Also update the README.md if it contains any relevant installation or troubleshooting instructions to mention this Python 3.13+ requirement.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: It keeps saying pydub not installed but it is

Assistant: Now I can see the issue. Looking at the code in `generate_voices.py`, here's what's happening:

## The Problem

The script detects `pydub` at **import time** (lines 82–87) by setting a global `HAS_PYDUB` flag:

```python name=tools/generate_voices.py url=https://github.com/SilvertongueRED/KIF-Pokedex-Voice-Over/blob/2dc4bf32850c3ffdf09edb32675b3faa695d1597/tools/generate_voices.py#L82-L87
try:
    from pydub import AudioSegment
    from pydub.effects import normalize
    HAS_PYDUB = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_PYDUB = False
```

Even though `pydub` the Python package is installed, the import can **silently fail** if **ffmpeg is not installed or not on your PATH**. When `pydub` loads, it tries to locate `ffmpeg`/`ffprobe`, and some versions or configurations raise an `ImportError` or a downstream error during the import of submodules when ffmpeg is missing.

This causes `HAS_PYDUB` to be `False`, and when the script runs it prints (line 633–638):

> `WARNING: pydub is not installed — Dexter audio effects will be skipped.`

## The Fix

**Install ffmpeg and make sure it's on your system PATH:**

| OS | Command |
|---|---|
| **Windows** | Download from [ffmpeg.org](https://ffmpeg.org/download.html), extract, and add the `bin/` folder to your system PATH. Or use `choco install ffmpeg` / `winget install ffmpeg`. |
| **macOS** | `brew install ffmpeg` |
| **Linux (Debian/Ubuntu)** | `sudo apt install ffmpeg` |

After installing ffmpeg, verify it works:
```bash
ffmpeg -version
```

Then re-run the script — `pydub` should import successfully and `HAS_PYDUB` will be `True`.

## How to confirm this is the issue

You can test it quickly in Python:

```bash
python -c "from pydub import AudioSegment; from pydub.effects import normalize; print('pydub OK')"
```

If this prints `pydub OK`, the script should work. If it throws an error mentioning ffmpeg/ffprobe, that confirms ffmpeg is the missing piece.

User: ffmpeg is in path.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Users\rolan\AppData\Roaming\Python\Python314\site-packages\pydub\utils.py", line 14, in <module>

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'audioop'

This is a **Python version incompatibility**, not a pydub installation problem.

You're running **Python 3.14**, which (along with 3.13) **removed the built-in `audioop` module** that pydub depends on ([PEP 594](https://peps.python.org/pep-0594/)). You can see pydub trying two things and both failing:

items.title: Fix Python 3.13 compatibility in requirements.txt
items.body:
## Problem

The build fails with "the configured Python interpreter version (3.13) is newer than PyO3's maximum supported version (3.12)". `fastapi==0.104.1` pulls in `pydantic-core==2.18.4`, which is built with PyO3 0.21.2 — a version that only supports up to Python 3.12.

## Solution

Upgraded `fastapi` to `0.115.0` and pinned `pydantic` to `2.9.2`, which pulls in `pydantic-core>=2.23.4`. That release uses PyO3 0.22+, which fully supports Python 3.13. Also bumped `uvicorn`, `httpx`, `typing-extensions`, and `urllib3` to contemporaneous stable versions, and removed the duplicate `asyncio` entry.

### Changes
- **Modified** `requirements.txt`

items.title: chore: bootstrap repository infrastructure
items.body:
## 概述
引导 `lifeos` 的仓库基础设施：初始化 Python 包骨架，补齐本地校验、依赖审计、发布流程与治理文档，并将支持版本范围扩展到 Python 3.10、3.11、3.12、3.13。

## 按模块变更
### 项目骨架
- 新增 `pyproject.toml`、`uv.lock` 与最小 `src/lifeos` 包结构
- 新增基础测试用例，补齐 `README.md` 开发说明与项目政策入口
- 将包元数据中的最低支持版本调整为 Python 3.10
- 为包元数据声明 Apache-2.0 许可证

### 本地质量门禁
- 新增 `.pre-commit-config.yaml`
- 引入 `ruff`、`mypy`、`detect-secrets` 与脚本校验
- 新增 `scripts/doctor.sh` 与 `scripts/lint.sh`
- 将 `mypy` 基线调整为 Python 3.10

### 依赖审计
- 新增 `scripts/dependency_health.sh`
- 在 CI 中加入 runtime `pip-audit`
- 新增定时依赖审计 workflow
- 将 `dependency-review` 调整为最低支持版本 Python 3.10 基线，以覆盖 3.10 专属依赖标记，而不是在每个 Python 版本重复运行同类审计

### CI 与版本兼容
- 保留 Python 3.13 作为默认质量门禁环境
- 新增 Python 3.10 / 3.11 / 3.12 运行矩阵验证
- 保持“完整质量门禁只跑一次、运行兼容性按版本矩阵验证”的结构，避免重复执行只需一次的检查
- 更新锁文件以覆盖 Python 3.10+ 的依赖解析结果

### 发布流程
- 新增 tag 驱动与手动触发的发布 workflow
- 增加构建产物版本与 tag 一致性校验
- 增加发布目标必须可从 `main` 到达的校验
- 在 CI 环境下强制 `uv sync --frozen`，避免发布过程漂移

### 仓库治理
- 新增 `LICENSE`、`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`、`CONTRIBUTING.md`、`SECURITY.md`
- 更新 `AGENTS.md` 以匹配当前仓库的验证、发布与文档约束

## 验证
- `rm -rf .venv && uv sync --all-extras --python 3.10 --frozen && .venv/bin/python -m pytest`
- `rm -rf .venv && uv sync --all-extras --python 3.11 --frozen && .venv/bin/python -m pytest`
- `rm -rf .venv && uv sync --all-extras --python 3.12 --frozen && .venv/bin/python -m pytest`

items.title: Adjust dependency pins for Python 3.14 compatibility (add version markers and pillow)
items.body:
### Motivation

- The app was failing to boot in newer Python runtimes because some pinned scientific packages and transitive `pillow` were being built from source and failing due to missing system libraries (e.g., `zlib`).
- The goal is to avoid source builds on Python 3.13+ by selecting wheel-friendly package ranges while preserving existing Python 3.11 pins.

### Description

- Updated `requirements.txt` to add Python-version-aware constraints for `numpy`, `pandas`, and `matplotlib` so older pins remain for Python 3.11 and relaxed wheel-friendly ranges apply for Python >= 3.13.
- Added an explicit `pillow>=11,<12` requirement so modern runtimes pick prebuilt wheels and avoid `zlib` compile failures when building from source.
- Added a short note to `README.md` explaining why the version markers were introduced and how they help on Python 3.14+ deployments.

### Testing

- Ran `python -m compileall app.py` and it completed successfully.
- Attempted `python -m pip install -r requirements.txt` but full dependency installation could not be validated because package index access is blocked in this environment (proxy returned `403 Forbidden`).

## Problem

The build fails during dependency installation because aiogram 3.7.0 pulls in pydantic-core 2.18.4, which depends on PyO3 0.21.2 — a version that does not support Python 3.13. Setting `python = "3.12"` in `railpack.toml` did not take effect, so the runtime remains Python 3.13.

Bumped aiogram from 3.7.0 to 3.8.0 in requirements.txt. aiogram 3.8.0 ships with a newer pydantic-core that includes pre-built wheels for Python 3.13, resolving the PyO3 incompatibility and allowing the dependency installation to succeed.

items.title: Fix CI: Python 3.10 compatibility and black formatting drift
items.body:
Two CI failures on the `copilot/fix-module-not-found-error` branch: `datetime.UTC` (Python 3.11+) caused `ImportError` on the Python 3.10 runners, and 9 files had accumulated Black formatting drift.

## Changes

- **`datetime.UTC` → `timezone.utc`** across all affected files — `datetime.UTC` was introduced in Python 3.11; replaced with `timezone.utc` (available since Python 3.2) to restore 3.10 compatibility:
  ```python
  # Before (Python 3.11+ only)
  from datetime import UTC, datetime
  return datetime.now(UTC)

  # After
  from datetime import datetime, timezone
  return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  ```
  Files affected: `auth/model.py`, `finance/models.py`, `finance/routes.py`, `finance/service.py`, `tests/test_finance.py`

- **Black formatting** — ran `black .` to bring all 9 drifted files into conformance with the enforced style check

fix: drop Pillow for Python <=3.7 to resolve remaining Dependabot alerts
items.body:
## Summary

Removes the Pillow==9.5.0; python_version <= '3.7' line from dev-requirements.txt, resolving the 4 remaining open Dependabot alerts (#29, #30, #31, #32).

## Why

All 4 remaining alerts require Pillow 10.x fixes, but Pillow 10.0 dropped Python 3.7 support. There is no version of Pillow that both supports Python 3.7 and fixes these CVEs:

- Alert #32: buffer overflow, fixed in 10.3.0
- Alert #31: arbitrary code execution, fixed in 10.2.0
- Alert #30: denial of service, fixed in 10.0.0
- Alert #29: libwebp OOB write, fixed in 10.0.1

Python 3.7 reached end-of-life in June 2023. Dropping the constraint means Pillow will simply not be installed in Python 3.7 dev environments, which is acceptable given EOL status.

items.title: Local-first Python 3.11 bootstrap and runtime provisioning for UniRig
items.body:
### Motivation
- Evitar la dependencia de un Python 3.11 global para el bootstrap de UniRig y permitir que la extensión instale/provisione un Python 3.11 localmente cuando no exista uno apropiado.
- Priorizar la reutilización del Python standalone que ya trae Modly, soportar un fallback que provisione un Python standalone dentro del runtime y ofrecer overrides offline/local-first.
- Mejorar trazabilidad y mensajes de error para explicar por qué se aceptó/rechazó cada candidato y para dejar claro que Blender Python no se usa para el bootstrap.

### Description
- Reescribí la resolución de Python 3.11 con prioridad local-first: overrides explícitos (`MODLY_UNIRIG_PYTHON311_BIN` y settings), detección del Python bundled de Modly, provisioning de un Python standalone dentro del runtime (`<runtime_root>/python311`), luego `py -3.11` y finalmente `python3.11`/`python` desde `PATH`.
- Añadí detección heurística del Python bundled de Modly con `_find_modly_bundled_python` y candidatos en `resources/python-embed/...` (Windows y Linux/macOS), tomando en cuenta variables de entorno (`MODLY_RESOURCES_DIR`, `MODLY_APP_DIR`, `MODLY_BACKEND_DIR`) y rutas de empaquetado/desarrollo.
- Implementé provisioning local de Python standalone con `_ensure_runtime_python311` que descarga/extracta el paquete de `python-build-standalone` (por defecto `3.11.9`, release `20240726`) dentro de `runtime/python311`; soporta overrides `MODLY_UNIRIG_PYTHON_STANDALONE_ARCHIVE` y `MODLY_UNIRIG_PYTHON_STANDALONE_URL` y extrae `.tar.gz` o `.zip`.
- Creé utilidades de verificación y descarga: `_probe_python_version`, `_download_file`, `_extract_archive`, `_find_python_in_tree` y modifiqué el flujo de `venv`/bootstrap para usar el Python resuelto y un wrapper `_pip_install_command` que respeta `MODLY_UNIRIG_WHEELHOUSE_DIR` (offline wheelhouse).
- Extendí `bootstrap_state.json` y trazabilidad: añadí campos `selected_python`, `selected_python_version`, `selected_python_source`, `python_lookup_attempts`, `standalone_python_root` e `install_phases`, y registré fases con `_append_install_phase`.
- Mensajes y validaciones aclaradas para exigir "standalone Python 3.11" y dejar explícito que "Blender Python is not used for this bootstrap"; se rechazan versiones (por ejemplo 3.14) con razones detalladas.
- Manteniendo el bootstrap aislado, preservé la instalación de `torch`, `spconv`, `torch-scatter/cluster`, `numpy==1.26.4` y `scipy`, y añadí soporte para índices/overrides actuales de paquetes (env vars existentes).
- Bump de `BOOTSTRAP_VERSION` y `manifest.json` (`0.1.2`) y añadido `CHANGELOG.md` con resumen y comandos QA.
- Archivos principales cambiados: `generator.py`, `tests/test_runtime_lifecycle.py`, `manifest.json`, y `CHANGELOG.md`.

### Testing
- Ejecuté los tests unitarios: `python -m unittest tests/test_runtime_lifecycle.py`, que corrieron los 7 tests implementados y terminaron con `OK`.
- Tests añadidos (unitarios/mocks): detección de Python bundled de Modly, fallback a Python standalone local, rechazo de Python 3.14 con mensaje que indica que "Blender Python is not used for this bootstrap", y persistencia de `selected_python_source` en `bootstrap_state.json`.
- Nota de alcance: la descarga/extracción real del standalone no se prueba end-to-end en CI; la lógica está cubierta por tests con mocks y se dejó soporte para overrides de archivo/URL para escenarios offline.

------

items.title: fix: correct DGL install version/URL and minimum Python version in README
items.body:
`dgl==2.3.0` does not exist on PyPI (latest is `2.2.1`), and the DGL 2.x wheel URLs changed to include the torch version prefix. Additionally, several packages in `requirements.txt` require Python ≥3.10, but the README instructed creating an environment with Python 3.9.

## Changes

- **Python env**: `conda create` target bumped `python=3.9` → `python=3.10`
- **DGL version**: `2.3.0` → `2.2.1` (both CPU and GPU install commands)
- **DGL wheel URLs**: updated to the current `torch-2.2` path format:
  ```bash
  # CPU
  pip install dgl==2.2.1 -f https://data.dgl.ai/wheels/torch-2.2/cpu/repo.html
  # CUDA 11.8
  pip install dgl==2.2.1 -f https://data.dgl.ai/wheels/torch-2.2/cu118/repo.html
  ```

- **Python 3.14 support**: Added to CI matrix (lint + test jobs) with `allow-prereleases: true`, added classifier to pyproject.toml, updated README
- **Security**: Removed insecure `RSA_1024` from valid key algorithms in ACM client

- **Tests**: Added 2 new tests for CA ARN filtering (describe fallback and error handling), updated existing tests. 400 tests pass with 87.65% coverage

items.title: Cross‑platform bootstrap script with embedded Python 3.14
items.body:
Cross‑platform bootstrap script with embedded Python 3.14
Attempts to download the native portablemc binary, falls back to pip.
Run with any Python (e.g., system 3.11) to set up and launch the launcher.

This main.py file will run "launch.py" will try the following things:
1. Find the absolute path of python 3.14 ".\python\python.exe"
2. Set user variables to the absolute path of python 3.14 if found
3. If not found, attempt to download python 3.14 from "https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.14.3/python-3.14.3-embed-amd64.zip"
4. Extract the embed.zip and rename the folder directory to "python"
5. Fix "python314._pth" by enabling "site-packages"
6. Download "get-pip.py" by `curl -o get-pip.py https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py`
7. Run "get-pip.py" via the embedded python at "/python/python.exe" using `/python/python.exe get-pip.py --trusted-host=files.pythonhosted.org --trusted-host=pypi.org`
8. Upgrade the pip by `/python/python.exe -m pip install --upgrade pip` and use "--user" argument if required
9. Install project dependencies: `python -m pip install flask flask-socketio psutil ansi2html portablemc`
10. Using the absolute path found for python 3.14 (see step 1), add the path to the SYSTEM environment variables as FIRST PROIRITY, but without administrator permission, fallback to the USER environmental variables in step 11 
11. Using the absolute path found for python 3.14 (see step 1), add the path to the USER environment variables as FIRST PROIRTY.
12. Run the launcher of "portablemc.py" via embedded python in the same process using environment `__compat_layer = runasinvoker` with embedded.

## Summary by Sourcery

Introduce a cross-platform bootstrapper that provisions an embedded Python runtime and launches the web-based PortableMC launcher, which now supports both native binary and Python module execution modes.

New Features:
- Add a main bootstrap script that downloads and configures an embedded Python 3.14 environment, installs required packages, and starts the launcher.
- Support using a native portablemc binary when available, falling back to the Python portablemc module via pip if needed.

Enhancements:
- Rename and adjust the launcher script to work with the new bootstrap flow and to adapt its command-line arguments depending on whether the portablemc binary or module is used.
- Streamline server-side logging, event handling, and output formatting while preserving existing web socket and streaming behavior.

Upgrade to Python 3.14
items.body:
Upgrades the minimum supported Python version from 3.11 to 3.14. The codebase required no runtime changes — it was already written using modern union syntax (`X | Y`), built-in generics (`list[...]`, `dict[...]`), and avoids all deprecated `typing` aliases.

## Configuration changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: `requires-python = ">=3.14"`, ruff `target-version = "py314"`, mypy `python_version = "3.14"`
- **`uv.lock`**: re-resolved against CPython 3.14.3; `tomli` dropped (stdlib since 3.11)

## Documentation

Added `python-314-upgrade.md` covering the compatibility assessment, specific Python 3.14 language changes reviewed (PEP 649, removed `typing` aliases, `asyncio` policy deprecations), and test results.

items.title: Fix torch/Python incompatibility on Intel Mac
items.body:
PyTorch dropped Intel Mac (x86_64) support in 2.5.0, and torch 2.4.x (the last Intel-compatible version) only ships wheels for Python ≤3.12.

- pyproject.toml: pin torch<2.5.0 for x86_64, unconstrained for arm64
- pyproject.toml: scope parakeet-mlx to arm64, faster-whisper to x86_64 (avoids pulling in heavy unused deps for each platform)
- install_service.sh: detect arch at install time — use Python 3.13 for Apple Silicon, Python 3.12 for Intel (satisfies torch 2.4.x cp312 wheels)
- install_service.sh: detect and recreate venv if it was built with the wrong Python version (handles the case where 3.13 venv already exists)

items.title: Fix release: pin Python 3.11-3.13 to avoid PyO3 3.14 incompatibility
items.body:
## Why

Release builds fail on all platforms because `--find-interpreter` discovers Python 3.14 on CI runners, and PyO3 0.22 only supports up to 3.13.

## What changed

- Replaced `--find-interpreter` with explicit `-i python3.11 -i python3.12 -i python3.13` flags
- Install all three Python versions via `actions/setup-python` with multi-version syntax
- Builds wheels for exactly the versions we support, no more

## Testing

- [x] Previous runs confirmed 3.11/3.12/3.13 wheels build successfully before hitting 3.14
- [x] After merge, delete `v0.1.1` tag, re-tag, and push to verify
